message:
refactor: fix stale docstring + clippy lints under --all-targets --all-features

Fresh-eyes review of 4a4e90c surfaced:

1) Stale docstring on `dispatch_sync_subcommand`: claimed "runs in a
   closure so an early return still yields a Result<()>", but the IIFE
   was removed during extraction — the function uses plain early
   returns now. Rewrote the comment to describe the actual contract
   (status branch reads-only, other branches hold &mut on
   open_result.storage, errors propagate to finalize_sync_result).

2) `cargo clippy --release` (without `--all-features`) was reporting
   clean, but CI runs `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features`,
   which flips on the `mcp` feature. That brought in
   `src/mcp/tools.rs` and the test tree, where four additional
   pre-existing lints were waiting:

   - `too_many_lines` on two `fn call` trait impls in mcp/tools.rs
     (113 and 122 lines). These are the `UpdateIssueTool` and
     `ManageDependenciesTool` entry points — their bodies thread
     argument parsing, coercion warnings, optional side-channel
     operations (labels, comments), and response-shape logic through
     one fn each. Extraction would fragment shared argument parsing
     for a marginal line-count win, so annotated with
     `#[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)]` and a comment explaining why.
   - Three `useless_conversion` warnings on `McpError::with_data(...)
     .into()` where source and target were both `McpError`.
   - Two `collapsible_if` warnings (`if let Some(comment) … { if
     !comment.is_empty() … }`) collapsed to let-chain form.
   - Two `too_many_lines` errors in tests/e2e_errors.rs —
     `e2e_sync_rebuild_preserves_unflushed_tombstones_across_delegation`
     (156 lines, my Round 15 test) and
     `e2e_sync_rename_prefix_clears_duplicate_external_ref_after_missing_db_recovery`
     (112 lines, pre-existing). Both annotated with
     `#[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)]` — e2e tests are intentionally
     long linear setup/action/assert sequences; extracting helpers
     scatters the scenario and hurts debuggability.

`cargo clippy --release --all-targets --all-features` exits 0 with
zero errors and zero warnings. `cargo fmt --check` clean.

Suite: lib 1240, e2e_errors 199, e2e_sync_artifacts 147,
e2e_sync_failure_injection 151, e2e_workspace_commands 172,
repro_3way_merge_bug 138 (2047 total, all green).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -241,9 +241,11 @@ fn maybe_delegate_rebuild(
 
 /// Dispatch to the appropriate sync-subcommand implementation based on
 /// the flag pattern (`--status` / `--flush-only` / `--merge` /
-/// default-or-`--import-only`). Runs in a closure so an early `return`
-/// inside the status branch still yields a `Result<()>` the caller can
-/// route through `finalize_sync_result`.
+/// default-or-`--import-only`). The status branch is read-only; the
+/// other three hold a `&mut` borrow on `open_result.storage` for the
+/// duration of their execution. Any `Err` propagates back to
+/// `finalize_sync_result`, which is the single place that decides how to
+/// handle recovery-backup rollback.
 fn dispatch_sync_subcommand(
     args: &SyncArgs,
     cli: &config::CliOverrides,
